trigger: vitamin D exceeds 100 mcg/day or 4,000 IU/day
proposed_action: flag high-dose intake and ask about calcium use, hypercalcemia, kidney stones, and lab monitoring
evidence_basis: NIH ODS vitamin D fact sheet

trigger: supplemental calcium around 1,000 mg/day or higher, especially with renal stone history
proposed_action: separate food calcium from supplemental calcium and check total intake

trigger: adult B6 intake exceeds 12 mg/day or approaches 100 mg/day
proposed_action: use dual threshold warning for neuropathy risk and route to manual review for chronic use
evidence_basis: EFSA vitamin B6 UL opinion and NIH ODS vitamin B6 fact sheet

trigger: supplemental elemental magnesium exceeds 350 mg/day or renal impairment is reported
proposed_action: flag diarrhea and hypermagnesemia risk context, with severity higher in renal impairment

trigger: iron intake is 45 mg/day or higher without treatment indication
proposed_action: flag GI adverse effects and separate therapeutic prescription use from self-use

trigger: zinc exceeds 40 mg/day or chronic high-dose use is reported
proposed_action: flag copper deficiency, GI symptoms, and medication spacing issues

trigger: preformed vitamin A exceeds 3,000 mcg RAE/day
proposed_action: distinguish retinol from beta-carotene and flag toxicity risk
evidence_basis: NIH ODS vitamin A fact sheet

trigger: high-dose vitamin E with anticoagulant or antiplatelet use
proposed_action: flag possible bleeding risk context and ask about medication co-use
evidence_basis: NIH ODS vitamin E fact sheet and anticoagulant safety materials

trigger: vitamin K supplement or abrupt intake change in warfarin user
proposed_action: warn against sudden intake changes and recommend INR monitoring discussion
evidence_basis: NIH ODS vitamin K fact sheet
